raw_input:
[5.0/5] TOTALLY works! I'm a low-maintenance girl and really not that into hair accessories, but I saw these for $3 at the store and decided to give them a try. I am BLOWN AWAY by how well these work!

I have long, thick hair. I pulled my hair up like I was going to create a high ponytail, but I did NOT put in an elastic. Instead, I just started coiling it into a bun. Once done coiling it, I spun the first pin through the base of the bun to hold the end of my hair down. Then I spun the other pin across through the body of the bun to hold its shape. I let go and IT DIDN'T BUDGE A BIT. It stayed exactly in place and did not try to uncoil. It stayed neat and in place even when I bumped the bun pretty hard getting into my car.

It took less than a minute from start to finish. SO COOL! [5.0/5] freakin awesome! I love this thing!!
I used to put my hair up in buns with hair ties, but I'd always end up redoing them in the middle of the day. This spin pin is SO GREAT--it doesn't get loose (especially if you put them in while your hair is damp/wet after a shower)! I'm sitting here with them in right now shaking my head around and they're not moving or getting loose at all! What's more is that they're really easy to use and I have mine in my hair in about 10 seconds. No joke.
Really great product!!! [1.0/5] False advertising by "NotJustWigs" The item I received is clearly not the Goody Simple Styles product advertised by "NotJustWigs". I did receive functional spin pins, but they are black instead of the brown color shown on the product image as well as [...]

More importantly, the spin pins I received have sharp, pointed ends without the rubberized protectors shown on the Goody Simple Styles product. These unprotected ends are sharp enough to cut skin if used without caution. I have a feeling this merchant is buying a cheaper, lower-quality product and marketing it under a higher quality brand name at a higher price. Bad form, NotJustWigs, bad form. [5.0/5] A few of these will take the place of a half dozen bobby pins I love these spin-pins. They really work. I purchased two boxes, and it usually takes 3 of these in my heavy, full hair to keep it all up and secure, but once I get them in they stay all day. It takes a few times to get used to using them, but once you get the hang of it, they are so easy to use. One of the best hair accessory inventions I've ever seen. [5.0/5] AWESOME! MUST HAVE FOR LONG HAIR! i have really long straight hair that seems to always be in the way. my hair is 26 inches from the top of the back of my head to the end of the hair. two spin pins hold it pretty well, making two buns would probably work better.

it would be niece if hair accessories came in a redish color for us redheads that are always left out. [5.0/5] Better than straight pins I don't know what it is about my hair, but straight pins just work their way out of my hair during the course of the day. And this is just from sitting at the office, not head-banging or anything. These Spin Pins stay put, and my hair stays where I put it. [1.0/5] Not authentic Goody product. Beware: if the item isn't packaged with the Goody label, it's not the real thing!

The item received wasn't the Goody brand. It was a poor imitation of the original, which is unfortunate, because I really wanted to replace the pair that I lost. The pins were packaged in a thin, clear plastic bag encased in an ivory mesh bag - clearly not Goody. I don't automatically have a problem with buying imitation products, but these simply weren't well made. I could see and feel that the end points were jagged and not cut smoothly, but attempted to use them anyway. I immediately noticed the difference in quality. They're supposed to glide through your hair when you spin them in, but since the points weren't buffed down properly, you could feel it ripping through instead. I had to return the item, which sounds easy enough. However, one has to still spend time packaging it, going to the post office, and waiting for/following up on the email confirmation that the item was returned successfully to the warehouse and refunded. I have Amazon Prime and this is one of those rare occasions when an order goes wrong. But it's still annoying enough to take the time to write about and to let the company know to not replace the genuine item with generic products. Needless to say, I still need a pair of *Goody* spin pins. [5.0/5] I love these spin pins. Such a great concept. Whoever created these pins are genius. :) Seriously what a brilliant concept! I absolutely love these pins. They're by far my favorite accessories. So light and so invisible yet does a great job holding my hair all day. Buy 2 or more. You won't regret it. [5.0/5] Just add them to your cart Do you put your hair into a bun? Or like, any kind of updo? Why don't you own these? These are my favorite hair product ever. I have very long curly hair and I wear these like everyday. One of these is the equivalent of like 10 bobby pins. They're rather expensive so I usually try to watch for them on sale at local stores. But through the years I've lost a couple and ordered from amazon. These are the BEST! [5.0/5] These rock!! Tried these on a whim. I have thick hair and regular bobby pins are like ammunition when putting my hair up - they eventually fly out... not the mention the inevitable headache that shows up. These pins twist in. I can now wear my hair up for as long as I want. Hair stays and no headache - all good!! [4.0/5] Pretty cool! I have to keep my hair up for work but tight buns and heavy hair pieces give me a headache. I can use these two pieces with nothing else and keep a secure bun all day with no headache. I usually put my hair into a lose french braid (only about 4 cross overs) twist the braided tail into a bun and put one pin in the top and one in the side. I do think crossing the pins works better than one in the top and one in the bottom.

I have used it with wet or dry hair, it is a little more stable with wet hair.

My hair is fairly thin, I think thicker hair would need more pins. My hair is only about 8" past my shoulders. I have run with it up and it stays without a problem, then the nice thing is I can nap in them too, they ae not too heavy or bulky that they hurt your head. [5.0/5] Works great I Love this product. It keeps heavy thick hair up in buns with ease! Though sometimes I will use 3 for extra security. [5.0/5] Absolutely amazing! I am a bobby pin junkie. Go into any room in our house and you will find a bobby pin:). Well, these little pins changed my life. I have quite thick curly hair and these will hold all day, even during a hard workout. I recommend these to everyone! [2.0/5] Not Good These were some generic brand and not real Goody brand spin pins. Worked fine but not as advertised in description. [3.0/5] doesn't work well enough I have decently thick hair and these pins cannot hold my hair up for even a minute. I have to use them after putting in a scrunchie. Meh. [5.0/5] Must try I have long, smooth asian hair. When ever I have a bun, I have to constantly redo it because it falls out. These actually do work! My bun stayed secure even through an exercise class. Amazing! I saw the negative reviews of the sharp edges....my pins had the balls at the ends. Maybe one vendor is selling an old/cheaper version. I would definitely recommend if you put your hair in a bun. It takes 5 seconds to twist it in your hair, very quick and easy. [5.0/5] I love these and I hope they never stop being made The only way my slippery hair will stay up!!! I love these and I hope they never stop being made!!! [5.0/5] GREAT for long, thick hair I really didn't believe this would work for my hair. I have smooth, layered, almost waist length hair (very thick), and it worked great! Just one will hold my hair, but I use 2 to make sure it holds all day. [1.0/5] False description I purchased these from NotJustWigs and am extremely disappointed. The picture and description lead you to believe that you will receive a brand new product. The product I received did not come in a box as pictured in the description (they came in little bags tied with a ribbon), and the original ends appear to have been cut off, so they are sharp. I have bought many of these pins in a regular store so I know what these are supposed to look like. The picture is not at all an accurate representation of what I received. I don't recommend buying this from NotJustWigs if you're looking for brand new Goody Spin Pins. Try a different seller or just buy them from a regular store.

response:
Spin pins work great most people. Hold thick, heavy, long, curly, slippery hair all day. Replace many bobby pins. Fast, easy use - seconds put hair up. Light, no headache. Many love, call best hair accessory. Some need 2-3 pins for thick hair. Work wet or dry hair.

Bad reviews mostly about seller NotJustWigs. Sent fake or wrong product - generic brand, wrong color, sharp unprotected ends instead real Goody brand. Real Goody pins have rubber tips, glide smooth. Fake ones feel rough, rip hair. Some buyers very angry, had to return. Recommend buy from different seller or regular store if want real Goody brand.
